notes: Use this plan with scripts/dolphin-gdb-trace.mjs --plan. It intentionally separates state, archive, high-level audio cue, and low-level AFS sinks.
High confidence requires a short capture window plus LR/caller correlation; do not infer semantic asset use from a lone low-level sink hit.

groups.name: asset-challenge-state
groups.event_window: Run from main-menu Challenge selection through one battle and result transition. This identifies which original state function owns the visible frontend screen.

breakpoints.event: Challenge state-table dispatch; capture current LR/caller cadence around screen transitions.
Challenge menu selection polling.
Load Box state.
Force/team/stage battle setup before battle entry.
Battle load/wait state.
Battle running state.
Post-battle result transition.
Challenge clear branch.
Challenge fail branch.
Challenge stage byte/subtable/variant selection.

groups.name: asset-archive-flow
groups.event_window: Run only while entering a screen or stage whose UI/HSD assets need proof. Keep max-hits low and trigger one transition per capture.

breakpoints.event: Disc file open; r3 is expected to point at a path/name.
Async disc read after a file has opened.
Filesystem table/name load boundary.
Game wrapper around HSD archive parse/public address lookup.

breakpoints.symbol: zz_004d548_ / secondary HSD archive parse wrapper
breakpoints.event: Secondary game wrapper around HSD archive parse.
Raw HSD archive parse import.
Raw HSD public symbol lookup; r4 often points at the public name.

groups.name: asset-audio-high
groups.event_window: Use during a single action/menu/event to map original cue id requests and their LR callers. This is the first audio pass.

breakpoints.event: High-level SFX request; r3 is actor or 0, r4 is cue id.
Sequence/audio continuation path. Noisy; use low max-hits.

groups.name: asset-audio-afs-sinks
groups.event_window: Use after asset-audio-high identifies an event. This resolves low-level AFS member/range arguments from regs and stack.

breakpoints.event: Low-level looped AFS start or AFS entry path; inspect regs and stack words for handle/index/range.
Low-level AFS start path; inspect regs and stack words for AFS index/range.
Audio switch/state path; useful for correlating handle state, not member identity alone.
